title: durable: Implement SQLite Journal Store
description: Create the core SQLite schema and JournalStore struct to persist workflow steps, handling serialization/deserialization of JSON state.

resolved: Use rusqlite for synchronous local SQLite operations
assumptions: SQLite is acceptable for local embedded use

ubiquitous: THE SYSTEM SHALL durably store step results in an SQLite database.

event_driven.shall: THE SYSTEM SHALL serialize the result and insert it into the journal

unwanted.condition: IF the journal file is corrupted
unwanted.shall_not: THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT crash ungracefully

postconditions: Step result is durable on disk
Can retrieve step by workflow_id and step_name
invariants: Journal is append-only for successful steps

happy: Write step result and read it back successfully
edge: Handle concurrent writes from multiple processes

phase_2: Implement JournalStore struct with insert/get methods

title: durable: Implement DurableCtx and ctx.run
description: Implement the DurableCtx struct that wraps the JournalStore and provides the `run` method to execute closures or return cached results.

resolved: Closures must return Result<T> where T is Serializable

ubiquitous: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide a DurableCtx to encapsulate workflow state

event_driven.trigger: WHEN ctx.run is called with a cache hit
event_driven.shall: THE SYSTEM SHALL return the cached result immediately without executing the closure

unwanted.shall_not: THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT record a successful step in the journal
unwanted.because: the step did not successfully complete

invariants: A step_name within a workflow_id must be unique

happy: Subsequent call returns cached result without executing closure
error: Closure returns error, is not cached

patterns: Future and closure bounds in Rust

phase_1: Implement the run method with proper trait bounds (Future, Serialize, Deserialize)
phase_2: Add unit tests proving cache hits skip execution

title: durable: Implement Saga Pattern (Compensation)
description: Add a compensation stack to DurableCtx. When a TerminalError occurs, execute registered rollbacks in reverse order.

ubiquitous: THE SYSTEM SHALL maintain a LIFO stack of compensation closures.

event_driven.trigger: WHEN a workflow encounters a TerminalError
event_driven.shall: THE SYSTEM SHALL execute all registered compensations in reverse order

unwanted.shall_not: THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT halt the entire compensation chain

postconditions: All registered compensations have been executed

happy: Register 3 compensations, return TerminalError, verify all 3 run in reverse
error: Compensation fails, next compensation still runs

phase_0: Add Vec<Box<dyn Future>> stack to DurableCtx

title: durable: Implement Idempotency and Virtual Object Locks
description: Implement lease-based locks in SQLite to ensure only one active workflow runs per target entity (Virtual Object pattern).

ubiquitous: THE SYSTEM SHALL prevent concurrent workflows on the same target ID

event_driven.shall: THE SYSTEM SHALL acquire a lease on the target ID

unwanted.condition: IF a workflow holds a lease and crashes
unwanted.shall_not: THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT deadlock forever

invariants: Only one active lease per Target ID at any time

error: Fail to acquire lease if held

title: durable: Refactor PipelineExecutor to DurableCtx
description: Rewrite the orchestrator PipelineExecutor to use the new DurableCtx, replacing manual while loops and persistence logic.

ubiquitous: THE SYSTEM SHALL execute the entire agent pipeline durably

event_driven.trigger: WHEN the executor resumes after a crash
event_driven.shall: THE SYSTEM SHALL fast-forward using ctx.run cache hits

unwanted.shall_not: THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT lose the generated code

error: Pipeline pauses and resumes successfully on simulated crash

phase_3: Wrap AgentDevelopment and Validation in ctx.run
